step_by_step_thinking:
The patient is a 10-year-old boy with a seizure disorder and a vascular plaque along the ophthalmic and maxillary divisions of the trigeminal nerve present since birth and unchanging morphologically. Given his description, especially noting the presence of a vascular plaque consistent with a port-wine stain along the trigeminal nerve division areas, the likelihood points towards **Sturge-Weber syndrome** given its classic features and the high correlation observed clinically.
\boxed{\text{A}}
